salesforce.observedModel: Record-centric workbench with tables, forms, related lists, hierarchy, and row-level or record-level actions.
transferablePrinciples: Tables are action surfaces, not passive reports.
Record forms separate view, edit, and create modes.
Hierarchy belongs in tree or tree-grid primitives when it changes navigation or permissions.

monday.observedModel: Board-centric work management with groups, columns, items, subitems, views, dashboards, widgets, filters, automations, and item cards.
transferablePrinciples: A work object should have multiple operational views.
Status and ownership columns are interaction primitives, not just decoration.
Dashboards should roll up source data and preserve drill-through.

microsoft365.observedModel: Document and task-pane workflows with command bars, tab bars, contextual ribbon commands, search, and narrow-pane navigation.
transferablePrinciples: Constrained panes need command bars and progressive navigation.
Icon-only controls require recognizable symbols and tooltips.
Contextual commands reduce clutter by appearing only when relevant.

googleWorkspace.observedModel: Cross-product workspace with side panels, inline smart chips, task capture, add-ons, and contextual entity previews.
transferablePrinciples: Users should keep the main artifact visible while consulting related apps.
Inline entity tokens can expose metadata and actions without opening a full detail page.
A side panel is ideal for capture and reference tasks.

fluentMaterial.observedModel: Design-system vocabulary for solving specific UI problems with components such as drawer, toolbar, nav, tree, chips, tags, toast, message bar, progress, and skeleton.
transferablePrinciples: Choose components by problem type and interaction cost.
Use compact semantic tokens for selection, filtering, and entity representation.
Differentiate blocking dialogs from supplemental popovers or drawers.
